name: Seoul
localTips: Get a T-money card at any convenience store — it covers the subway, buses and even taxis, and reloads at machines everywhere.
Winters go properly sub-zero with a biting wind chill — layer up, since most palaces, markets and hikes stay open regardless.
Tipping isn't expected anywhere, including restaurants and taxis — trying to leave one can actually confuse or embarrass staff.
Convenience stores (GS25, CU, 7-Eleven) have outdoor seating and hot food counters — a legitimate meal option at 3am when little else is open.
Download Naver Map or KakaoMap rather than relying on Google Maps — Google's turn-by-turn directions are heavily restricted in South Korea.

activities.title: Gyeongbokgung Palace guard ceremony
activities.description: Watch the Royal Guard Changing Ceremony in full Joseon-dynasty uniform at Gwanghwamun Gate, free to view several times a day in front of Seoul's largest palace.

activities.title: Bukchon Hanok Village
activities.description: Narrow alleys of 600-year-old traditional hanok houses between Gyeongbokgung and Changdeokgung — climb to Bukchon 8th viewpoint for the tiled-roof skyline.

activities.title: Insadong streets & tea houses
activities.description: A pedestrian strip of antique shops, calligraphy stores and traditional tea houses down alleys off the main road — Ssamziegil's spiral courtyard is the centrepiece.

activities.title: Myeongdong street food & shopping
activities.description: Seoul's densest street-food strip — skewered tteokbokki, egg bread and cheese corndogs sold between K-beauty flagship stores, loudest and busiest after dark.

activities.title: Namsan Park
activities.description: Wooded trails climb from Myeongdong to the base of N Seoul Tower — a free cable car alternative is the 30-minute walk past couples' love-lock fences.

activities.title: Hongdae street performances
activities.description: Buskers, K-pop dance crews and art students turn the streets around Hongik University into a free open-air show every weekend evening.

activities.title: Dongdaemun Design Plaza & night market
activities.description: Zaha Hadid's neofuturist steel curves light up in LED patterns after dark, with the surrounding Dongdaemun wholesale fashion market open into the early morning.

activities.title: Cheonggyecheon Stream walk
activities.description: A restored 11km stream running below street level through downtown — stepping stones, waterfalls and, in November, the Seoul Lantern Festival's floating displays.

activities.title: Yeouido Hangang Park
activities.description: Grassy riverside lawns where locals order fried chicken delivery straight to their picnic blanket — rent a bike or just watch the Banpo Bridge fountain across the water.

activities.title: Ihwa Mural Village
activities.description: Steep hillside stairways painted with murals of fish, flowers and giant staircases turn a former slum-clearance village into an open-air gallery above the city.

activities.title: Gwangjang Market
activities.description: Seoul's oldest covered market since 1905 — the bindaetteok (mung bean pancake) alley and raw beef tartare (yukhoe) stalls draw lines that snake between fabric shops.

activities.title: Noryangjin Fish Market
activities.description: A vast covered wholesale fish market where you buy live seafood at the stalls, then carry it upstairs to a restaurant that will prepare it for a small fee.

activities.title: Seokchon Lake & Lotte World Tower
activities.description: A cherry-blossom-lined lake circling Lotte World Tower, Korea's tallest building — the free lakeside path is the best angle for photographing the whole spire.

activities.title: Deoksugung Stonewall Walkway
activities.description: A romantic ginkgo-lined lane along the palace's stone wall, said to bring bad luck to couples who walk it — go for the autumn colour or the palace guard ceremony at the gate.

activities.title: Express Bus Terminal underground arcade
activities.description: A sprawling underground shopping arcade beneath Seoul's biggest bus terminal — the Goto Mall alone runs nearly a kilometre of stalls, entirely indoors and free to browse.

activities.title: 24-hour jjimjilbang bathhouse
activities.description: Communal hot-tub bathing floors below, heated sauna rooms shaped like kilns and igloos above — places like Siloam or Dragon Hill Spa never close.
activities.insiderNote: Bathing floors are gender-separated and nude; the sauna and sleeping floors upstairs are mixed and require the provided uniform — bring your own toiletries or buy a kit at the counter.

activities.title: Euljiro printing-district hofs
activities.description: Old-school beer halls (hofs) tucked between working print shops and hardware stalls in Euljiro's back alleys — mismatched furniture, no signage, cheap draft beer.
activities.insiderNote: Look for a plastic stool setup outside a metal shutter door around Euljiro 3-ga station — most have no name in English, just point and follow your nose.

activities.title: Pojangmacha tented street stalls
activities.description: Orange plastic tents that appear on sidewalks after dark selling soju, grilled skewers and spicy stir-fried squid — Jongno's stretch has the highest concentration.
activities.insiderNote: These are cash-friendly and informal — squeeze in at the shared bar counter and order by pointing at what your neighbours are eating.

activities.title: Hidden hanok courtyard cafe
activities.description: Restored traditional houses in Bukchon and Ikseon-dong hide courtyard cafes behind their gates — order a citron tea and sit under the tiled eaves.
activities.insiderNote: Ikseon-dong's alleys are dense with these but easy to walk past — look for a small wooden gate left slightly ajar, that's usually the sign it's open.

activities.title: Namdaemun Market
activities.description: Korea's oldest and largest traditional market, open since 1414 — a maze of covered alleys selling everything from imported ginseng to knock-off camping gear.
activities.insiderNote: The kalguksu (knife-cut noodle soup) alley near Gate 1 opens before dawn for market porters — a proper local breakfast for a few thousand won.

activities.title: Vinyl listening bar
activities.description: Basement bars in Yeonnam-dong and Itaewon built entirely around record collections — request a track, sip a whisky highball, and listen through vintage speakers.
activities.insiderNote: Most listening bars post a strict no-loud-talking policy — this is a music venue first, a bar second.

activities.title: 24-hour haejangguk hangover soup
activities.description: Ox-blood and cabbage soup built specifically to cure a soju hangover, served round the clock at institutions like Cheongjinok near Gwanghwamun since 1937.
activities.insiderNote: Order it before 8am and you'll be sitting among night-shift workers and taxi drivers rather than tourists — it's a genuinely local breakfast ritual.

activities.title: Seochon quiet hanok alleys
activities.description: West of Gyeongbokgung, Seochon's hanok lanes see a fraction of Bukchon's tour groups — poet Yun Dong-ju's old boarding house and small galleries hide along Ogin-gil.
activities.insiderNote: Start at Tongin Market's yeopjeon lunchbox stall, where you buy brass coins to trade for banchan across a dozen tiny stands, then wander north into the alleys.

activities.title: N Seoul Tower observatory
activities.description: Take the cable car up Namsan and ride the elevator to the digital observatory deck for a 360-degree skyline view — book the sunset-to-night slot for the best light.

activities.title: DMZ & Joint Security Area day tour
activities.description: A guided trip to the world's most fortified border — peer into North Korea from the JSA blue huts or descend into the Third Tunnel of Aggression.
activities.bookingQuery: DMZ Joint Security Area tour from Seoul

activities.title: Korean BBQ & soju tasting class
activities.description: Grill your own galbi and samgyeopsal with an instructor explaining the wrap-and-dip etiquette, paired with a flight of regional soju and makgeolli.
activities.bookingQuery: Korean BBQ soju tasting class Seoul

activities.title: Hanbok rental at Gyeongbokgung
activities.description: Rent a traditional hanok — hair styling included at most shops — and wear it around the palace, which waives its entrance fee for anyone dressed in hanbok.

activities.title: Nanta non-verbal comedy show
activities.description: A percussive kitchen-set comedy show performed with knives, pots and pans instead of words — Korea's longest-running theatre production, no language barrier at all.

activities.title: Han River sunset cruise
activities.description: A pleasure boat runs from Yeouido past the illuminated bridges of the Han River, with a rooftop deck for photos of the Banpo Bridge fountain show.

activities.title: Kimchi-making class
activities.description: Learn to salt, season and pack napa cabbage into kimchi with a local host, then take your jar home — some classes pair it with a full home-style Korean lunch.

activities.title: Everland theme park
activities.description: Korea's largest theme park — the T Express wooden rollercoaster, a safari zone of lions and tigers, and seasonal flower festivals across four themed zones.

activities.title: Temple stay experience
activities.description: Overnight or day programme at a working Buddhist temple like Jogyesa or Bongeunsa — monastic meals, tea ceremony and early-morning meditation with resident monks.

activities.title: Gyeongbokgung moonlight night tour
activities.description: A limited seasonal ticket opens the palace after closing hours, lit by lanterns with far smaller crowds than the daytime rush — books out within minutes of release.
